celebration: ch leaf #2 goes up on the Pattern Tree. CHICK JOINS — and her name is DECODABLE the day she arrives (ch-i-ck). The first cast member the class can spell at debut.

songs.title: Ch-Ch-Chick!
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
Ch! Ch! Ch-ch-ch!
(ch… ch… ch…)

[Hook]
Ch! Ch! Ch-ch-ch!
Two letters, ONE sound!
Ch-ch-chick! Ch-ch-chick!
It's a chick! (hello, Chick!)

[Verse 2]
Ch! Ch! Ch-ch-ch!
What begins with CH?
Ch-ch-chin! Ch-ch-chin!
On my chin! (my chin!)

[Verse 3]
Ch! Ch! Ch-ch-ch!
What begins with CH?
Ch-ch-chop! Ch-ch-chop!
Chop, chop, chop! (chop it up!)

[Verse 4]
Ch! Ch! Ch-ch-ch!
What begins with CH?
Ch-ch-cheese! Ch-ch-cheese!
I like cheese! (yum, cheese!)

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! Not CH!
(ch… ch… ch…)

[Outro]
Ch-ch-chick! Ch-ch-chick!
It's a chick! (a chick!)
Ch! Ch! Ch-ch-ch!
(chat, Chick, chat!)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: ch is the voiceless affricate /tʃ/ — the 'choo-choo train' sound — NEVER the letter names 'see-aitch' and NEVER 'chuh' with a schwa. The '(ch… ch… ch…)' lines are dry train-chuff /tʃ/ hits riding the hi-hats. 'Two letters, ONE sound' reprises the Week 27 lesson line. The hook welcomes cast member Chick — the first friend whose name the class can SPELL the day she debuts (ch-i-ck). Outro sends her off chatting into next week's song.

songs.title: Chick Can Chat
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(We can read. We can read!)

[Hook]
The chick can chat!
The chick can chat!
Chat, chat, chat! (chat! chat!)
The chick can chat!

[Verse 2]
Sheep sat on a mat.
Chick can chat and chat!
Chat, chat, chat! (so much chat!)
The chick can chat!

[Verse 3]
Sheep can nap. (shhh…)
Chick can chat much!
Chat, chat, chat! (Chick! Chick!)
Can Chick nap? (NO — chat!)

[Outro]
Chick can chat… (shhh…)
A potato?! (POTATO!)
Chick can not chat! (…at last!)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: Frame: 'The chick can chat.' — the anthem for the class's most talkative new friend. Verse 2 sets Sheep up as the patient listener; verse 3 Sheep tries to nap while Chick keeps going. The core word 'much' lands in verse 3. The joke pays off in the outro: the potato appears and the world's chattiest chick is, for once, speechless — the class shouts POTATO, then whispers the payoff line.

spreads.artNote: CAST DEBUT: Chick — a tiny fluffy yellow chick with a stubby orange beak — stands alone in the spotlight, beak already open mid-word. The children learn a name they can also SPELL: ch-i-ck.
Chick chatting brightly, tiny wings spread, a swirl of little speech puffs around her. Pure enthusiasm.
Three bold speech bubbles stacked, Chick beak wide, feathers vibrating with talk. Comedy of volume from such a small bird.
Chick pacing across the deck of a little stage, still chattering, a long ribbon of speech-puffs trailing behind her.
Sheep — a fluffy round white sheep with kind dark eyes — settles onto a cosy mat, eyes half-closed, hoping for peace and quiet.
Chick perched right on the edge of Sheep's mat, chattering into Sheep's ear. Sheep's eyes now fully open, patient but weary.

spreads.text: Sheep can nap. Chick can chat!
spreads.artNote: Sheep curled with eyes shut trying to nap; Chick standing on Sheep's woolly back, STILL chatting, a 'zzz' and a speech-puff colliding in the air.
The biggest chatter yet — Chick mid-leap with joy, a giant cloud of speech-puffs filling the spread. Sheep peeks out from under a wing, resigned and fond.

A single potato has appeared centre-stage in the spotlight. Chick stares at it, beak clamped shut for the first time ever, one eye wide. Sheep blinks in astonishment. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS it.

extras: PATTERN TREE: leaf #2. The class reads chin/chop/chat/much aloud and the ch leaf goes up beside sh. Two leaves now — the branch is growing.
Sandpaper digraph ch: trace the two letters as ONE gesture, one sound — the 'choo-choo train' sound, not the letter names.
EAL minimal-pair sort: ch vs sh — chip/ship, chop/shop, chin/shin, chew/shoe (oral). Mandarin-L1 children merge /tʃ/ and /ʃ/ without explicit contrast; do this sort slowly with a mirror.
Moveable alphabet: build ch welded, then c-h-i-n, c-h-o-p, c-h-a-t, m-u-ch (note ch can END a word too — 'much').

teacherFocus: THE SECOND PATTERN. ch is a brand-new sound made by two letters — the 'choo-choo train' sound /tʃ/. Teach the sandpaper digraph as one gesture, just like sh. Then celebrate CHICK: unlike Sheep (whose name waits until Week 44), Chick's name is fully decodable the day she arrives — ch-i-ck — so the class SPELLS their new friend's name on debut. Show that ch works at the END of a word too, with the core word 'much'. EAL priority: ch vs sh minimal pairs (chip/ship, chop/shop, chin/shin) — Mandarin-L1 children merge /tʃ/ and /ʃ/; use a mirror and go slow. Hang leaf #2 with ceremony.
